Embedded financial wellness platform providing banks and credit unions with white-label credit monitoring, credit building, identity protection, and financial health tools delivered via API; enables FIs to offer consumer fintech-grade financial health features natively within their digital banking apps.
Transaction data analytics and AI-powered customer intelligence for FI marketing teams; merchant categorization, life event detection, and behavioral segmentation

AI-powered permissioned data platform that accelerates income, employment, cash-flow, and stipulation verification for lenders. Uses ML and alternative data (bank data, permissioned payroll, etc.) to reduce manual workloads, improve pull-through rates, and approve more creditworthy applicants. Pre-fills applications and cross-walks data across SoS, IRS, DoD, SBA, Google.
Embedded SMB data analytics platform connecting QuickBooks, Xero, Salesforce, Stripe and 100+ SaaS apps to provide FIs with cash flow insights and cross-sell opportunities for business customers.
SMB cash flow analytics and next-best-product recommendation engine embedded in digital banking, giving business customers real-time financial insights and FIs actionable cross-sell leads.

subcategories.name: Model Governance & Explainability
subcategories.description: AI governance and explainability platforms provide model documentation frameworks, bias detection tooling, audit trail generation, explainability reporting (including FCRA adverse action notice support), and AI policy management for regulated financial institutions deploying AI in credit, fraud, and other decision-making contexts. Products help FIs demonstrate responsible AI use to regulators and examiners. Chief Risk Officers, compliance leaders, and Chief Data Officers are the buyers.
subcategories.ai_opportunities: As banking regulators increase scrutiny of AI in credit decisions, governance platforms that automatically generate examiner-ready explainability reports and monitor deployed models for bias drift are becoming essential infrastructure for community banks and credit unions that rely on third-party AI vendors and must demonstrate oversight to their regulators.

products.description: AI governance and model risk management platform with primary focus on financial services; 25+ pre-packaged SR 11-7, CECL, and ECOA compliance templates; automates model documentation, validation workflows, peer review, and ongoing monitoring; never stores model data for security; covers classical ML and generative AI

AI model observability and risk management platform for financial services; monitors ML model performance, drift, and fairness; generates explainability outputs for regulatory review; streamlines MRM and GRC workflows.

Agentic AI governance platform for regulated industries; automates model documentation, validation testing, and compliance workflow for SR 11-7, SS1/23, E-23, and EU AI Act; covers credit risk, fraud, and AML model governance.

AI governance platform covering model validation, bias detection, drift monitoring, and audit trails for regulated industries; covers both classical ML and generative AI models; banking, insurance, and healthcare vertical focus.

Private AI governance platform for financial institutions providing 100+ pre-built AI agents with built-in GRC and cybersecurity controls, complete audit trails, and full data sovereignty. Positioned for banks, credit unions, and wealth managers seeking to deploy AI without sharing data with third-party model providers.

AI fairness auditing platform for mortgage lenders. Detects bias across protected classes in automated decisioning models, provides CFPB-aligned explainability reports, and generates audit-ready adverse action documentation for fair lending examinations.
AI model fairness and bias detection toolkit; tests credit models for disparate impact and ECOA compliance before deployment

AI-native fair lending platform using ML to detect and remediate bias in credit models; evaluates equally predictive but less discriminatory model alternatives (LDAs) to reduce disparate impact; identifies variables driving protected class disparities; covers adverse action explanation, geographic fairness mapping, and model de-biasing.

Integrated AI platform combining predictive, generative, and agentic AI with governance native to every layer. Used by banks and credit unions for credit risk, fraud detection, collections optimization, and retention, with immutable audit trails and model explainability built in.

AI and ML model governance platform for financial institutions; automates the 3-4 week process of classifying, risk-scoring, validating, and scheduling review for every AI model deployed at an FI; maps existing model inventory to regulatory examination requirements; addresses the emerging examiner expectation for formal AI model governance programs

subcategories.name: Open Banking & Data Access
subcategories.description: Open banking and data access platforms enable permissioned sharing of consumer financial data between FIs and third-party applications via APIs, supporting use cases from account aggregation and financial planning to lending underwriting and payment initiation. Products in this category include data aggregators, open banking connectivity platforms, and APIs that enable data portability under evolving regulations including CFPB Rule 1033. IT leaders, digital banking teams, and compliance officers are the buyers.
subcategories.ai_opportunities: Open banking data access is the foundational layer for AI-powered cash flow underwriting, real-time financial wellness tools, and multi-institution financial management — enabling community financial institutions to participate in a connected financial services ecosystem rather than being bypassed by data aggregators that primarily serve fintech apps.

products.description: Enterprise open finance connectivity platform that enables financial institutions to replace screen scraping and credential sharing with FDX-standard API-based data sharing; provides both FDX-compliant open banking APIs and direct integrations into business applications (ERP, accounting, treasury management); positions as Section 1033 compliance infrastructure for community banks and credit unions facing CFPB open banking mandates; Plaid Preferred Partner for optimized FDX connectivity; integrated with Narmi digital banking platform (Sept 2025) and Candescent digital banking (April 2025, 1,300+ FI clients); enables consumer-permissioned data sharing with 3rd-party fintech apps without credential exposure

Consumer-permissioned financial data aggregation platform; aggregates account, transaction, asset, employment, and cash flow data from 99%+ of US financial accounts to power PFM, lending decisions, wealth management, and financial insights within FI digital banking applications.

Cloud-native modern core banking platform acquired by Fiserv in 2021. Built on AWS with a real-time ledger and open API layer. Positioned as Fiserv next-generation core for forward-looking community FIs.
Bank-owned open banking network enabling permissioned, tokenized financial data sharing between FIs and fintechs; replaces credential-based screen scraping
Open banking API developer portal enabling community FIs to connect fintech partners and third-party applications to FIS core systems. Supports open banking standards and fintech ecosystem access.
Cloud-native core banking platform built on Microsoft Azure. FIS next-generation architecture for community FIs seeking to modernize. Real-time processing with open API design.
Payroll connectivity API enabling frictionless direct deposit switching with 100% U.S. payroll coverage, including credential-less PreMatch technology for income verification.

Payroll connectivity API platform enabling financial institutions to offer direct deposit switching, payroll-linked lending decisions, and income verification; lets customers set up or switch their direct deposit destination in real time within the FI's digital banking app.

Payroll connectivity and direct deposit switching platform that enables financial institutions to help customers redirect their paycheck to a new account; covers 100% of US payroll providers with both credential-based and credential-less (PreMatch) switching options to drive primary account status.
Partner integration marketplace for CSI core banking clients; 67 vetted fintech solutions across lending, payments, digital banking, compliance, and card services.

Financial data aggregation and enrichment platform embedded in FI digital banking and PFM deployments; delivers ML-based transaction categorization (1,500+ merchant categories), spending trend analysis, cash flow labeling, net worth aggregation, and account-level financial health data feeds; FIs deploy Yodlee as the data enrichment layer powering their digital banking PFM, financial wellness tools, and AI-driven member insights; also enables permissioned data access to consumer-authorized external accounts
Open banking-based asset verification for mortgage; pulls live bank account data to replace the VOD process.
Open banking-based income and employment verification for loan origination; analyzes live payroll and bank data

API-first digital banking and open banking integration platform for community banks and credit unions; enables connectivity between fintech vendors and core banking systems (FIS, Fiserv, Jack Henry, CSI); 40+ core integrations; Fintech Connector module for B2B FI-to-vendor API management

ML-enriched transaction data product for financial institutions; cleans and categorizes raw bank transaction data with normalized merchant names, merchant category codes, geolocation data, and AI-assigned spending categories; used by FIs to power PFM features, cash flow analytics, and downstream AI use cases without building proprietary enrichment pipelines; also includes Plaid Signal for ACH return risk scoring

Integration-Platform-as-a-Service (iPaaS) for financial institutions, launched following nCino's acquisition of Sandbox Banking; provides prebuilt connectors for core banking platforms and 50+ industry solutions spanning lending, deposits, KYC/AML, underwriting, mobile banking, and analytics; supports 14+ core banking systems; low-code configuration for banks and credit unions.

Integration-Platform-as-a-Service (iPaaS) for financial institutions, launched September 2025 following nCino's acquisition of Sandbox Banking (formerly Glyue) for $52.5M in February 2025; provides prebuilt connectors for core banking platforms and 50+ industry solutions spanning lending, deposits, CCaaS, CRM, document management, KYC/AML, underwriting, mobile banking, and analytics; supports more than 14 core banking systems; low-code configuration enables banks and credit unions to build and run integrations without custom engineering; previously used by 100+ FIs under the Glyue/Sandbox Banking brand; COCC selected Sandbox Banking as iPaaS provider for its ConnectSuite platform prior to acquisition

Open-architecture credit union core banking platform. Built by ex-Symitar engineers; launched 2009. Features real-time processing, open database access, and a configurable data model attractive to mid-size CUs seeking flexibility over packaged systems.
Developer API platform enabling fintechs and FIs to customize UI across Jack Henry products; part of the Digital Toolkit alongside the Plugin Framework, Consumer API, and Admin API.

Cloud-based data exchange platform on Google Cloud providing FIs and fintechs bulk access to Jack Henry system data via defined schema subscriptions; complements jXchange transactional APIs with analytical data pipelines.

Banking-as-a-Service platform enabling community banks to run compliant fintech sponsor bank programs. Provides ledger, KYC/AML, card issuance, money movement, compliance monitoring, fraud controls, and dispute management via API. Centralizes risk management for sponsor banks through Synctera Console. Notable sponsor bank clients: Coastal Community Bank (27 fintech partnerships), Regent Bank.

Money API for fintech companies building payment applications on bank and credit union infrastructure. Supports ACH debit/credit, instant ACH, digital wallets, and bank-to-bank transfers. KYC/AML and digital wallet capabilities included.
Card-on-file switching API enabling bank customers to update their payment card at merchant subscription and billing sites directly from within their digital banking app.

subcategories.name: Banking as a Service (BaaS)
subcategories.description: Banking as a Service (BaaS) encompasses the platforms and chartered financial institutions that enable fintechs, brands, and non-bank companies to embed financial products — checking accounts, debit cards, credit products, and payments — via APIs using a community bank or credit union's banking charter. Products include middleware BaaS platforms that manage the fintech-to-sponsor-bank relationship, API-first chartered banks offering direct rails, and community banks operating structured sponsor banking programs. BaaS strategy decisions typically involve CEOs, boards, and technology leaders at sponsor institutions.
subcategories.ai_opportunities: AI-powered compliance monitoring and transaction surveillance are becoming critical infrastructure for community banks operating BaaS programs, as regulators expect sponsor banks to demonstrate real-time oversight of fintech partner transaction flows — an area where AI can provide the coverage breadth that manual review cannot at scale.

products.product_name: The Bancorp Fintech Solutions & Program Sponsorship
products.description: Comprehensive BaaS and program sponsorship platform from a publicly traded sponsor bank; services include BIN sponsorship, card issuing (prepaid, debit, virtual), ACH settlement, compliance oversight, and Small Business Administration lending; pioneered fintech card-issuing sponsorship in 2003; supports 150M+ active card accounts; operates as #1 U.S. issuer of prepaid cards and 6th largest debit card issuer overall; program categories include consumer prepaid, business prepaid, health savings accounts (HSA), earned wage access, and HELOC; fintech partners span neobanks, payroll processors, benefits platforms, and gig economy companies; Wilmington DE

products.product_name: Green Dot Bank BaaS & Embedded Finance
products.description: FDIC-insured sponsor bank providing embedded finance and BaaS rails for consumer-facing fintech programs; Green Dot Bank (Provo UT) is the sponsor bank entity backing major embedded programs including Walmart MoneyCard, Apple Cash (via Apple Pay), and earned wage access programs; Dayforce (Ceridian) selected Green Dot as BaaS partner for on-demand pay in 2025; programmatic banking division (FSC) manages B2B embedded finance partnerships; Q1 2026 results show continued BaaS program growth with new FSC partners being onboarded; announced Nov 2025 split: Smith Ventures acquiring fintech operations, CommerceOne acquiring Green Dot Bank with exclusive 7-year sponsor bank agreement; restructuring expected to close Q2 2026

Technology-forward bank providing BaaS infrastructure, loan origination, and embedded payments to 1,200+ fintech and enterprise partners; BaaS services span card issuing, ACH, wire, real-time payments, consumer lending origination, and BNPL infrastructure; Thredd partnership (Jan 2026) extends platform to international fintechs seeking U.S. bank sponsorship; Commercial Banking Group handles institutional credit with $2.4B AUM; CRB-X technology subsidiary provides proprietary risk analytics and lending infrastructure; API-driven partner onboarding with compliance oversight framework; Fort Lee NJ

End-to-end financial technology platform providing card issuing, payment processing, core banking, and BaaS infrastructure for fintechs and financial institutions; services include debit and prepaid card issuing, ACH, wire transfers (with community bank sponsor Fedwire access via Community Federal Savings Bank), real-time payments, credit products, and fraud/risk management APIs; Cyberbank Core provides modern core banking as a service enabling sponsor banking programs; powers Chime, Robinhood, Varo, Revolut (U.S.), TransferWise, Monzo (U.S.), KOHO, Paysafe, and others; SoFi adopted Galileo Cyberbank Core for commercial payment services sponsor banking program in 2024; rebranding to SoFi Tech Solutions; ABA Partner Network member
BaaS sponsor bank providing turnkey prepaid card, payments, and specialty lending programs via its regulatory infrastructure; formerly MetaBank, rebranded 2022.

Bank-direct embedded banking platform enabling community banks and credit unions to offer branded fintech partnerships at scale; BankOS console gives bank compliance teams a unified interface to manage embedded programs, third-party fintech relationships, transaction monitoring, and regulatory reporting; multi-bank network of 20+ FIs allows fintechs to apply once and gain access to multiple bank sponsor options; AI Marketplace (launched Dec 2025) curates 3,600+ fintech partners for bank program development; joined U.S. Bank Connected Partnership Network Oct 2025 for larger enterprise payment programs; first credit union admitted to network (People Trust Community FCU) in 2025

Q2 Helix is Q2's Banking-as-a-Service platform enabling banks and credit unions to power embedded finance programs for fintech and enterprise partners; provides programmable accounts, payments, and compliance infrastructure via API.

Banking-as-a-Service platform enabling community banks to run compliant fintech sponsor bank programs. Provides ledger, KYC/AML, card issuance, money movement, compliance monitoring, fraud controls, and dispute management via API. Centralizes risk management for sponsor banks through Synctera Console. Notable sponsor bank clients: Coastal Community Bank (27 fintech partnerships), Regent Bank.

Developer-first embedded finance platform enabling companies to launch FDIC-insured bank accounts, debit cards, ACH and wire payments, and credit products via APIs; relies on licensed bank sponsor partners for underlying banking services; provides KYC/AML, fraud monitoring, and compliance tooling for fintech program operators; serves enterprise fintech clients including Invoice2Go (Bill.com), Roofstock, and Relay Financial; "Deepening our commitment to banks" initiative (2024) pivoted platform toward enabling banks to white-label Unit's embedded finance infrastructure directly; 1.4M end-customers; 10M+ API calls/day

Nationally chartered bank (Column N.A.) offering direct API-based banking infrastructure without middleware; combines an OCC-issued national bank charter with a modern, API-first technology stack built from scratch; enables fintechs and non-bank enterprises to issue accounts, process payments (ACH, wire, FedNow, RTP), and originate loans through direct bank APIs rather than through a middleware BaaS layer; eliminates third-party BaaS intermediary fragmentation; key fintech clients include Mercury and Brex (partner banks); $153M estimated revenue 2025 (219% YoY growth); API documentation publicly available with developer-first onboarding
Cloud-native Banking-as-a-Service platform that licenses embedded finance infrastructure directly to community banks; integrates with SilverLake via jXchange.

Cloud-native, API-first core banking SaaS platform; enables financial institutions to configure banking products, integrate ecosystem partners, and evolve continuously on a composable architecture without legacy technology constraints; supports consumer, SME, and embedded finance use cases.

subcategories.name: Industry Research & Advisory
subcategories.description: Industry research and advisory covers analyst firms, research institutes, and trade associations that produce primary research, surveys, frameworks, and publications specifically shaping AI adoption decisions at community banks and credit unions. Products and services include subscription research, benchmarking studies, conference content, and advisory engagements. CEOs, Chief Strategy Officers, and innovation leaders at community financial institutions are the primary audience.
subcategories.ai_opportunities: Research firms and advisory organizations that can translate complex AI vendor claims into practical community bank use case frameworks — with benchmarking data from peer institutions — are providing unique value as community bank executives navigate an overwhelming volume of AI vendor marketing with limited internal technical resources to evaluate it.

products.description: Economic research, technology adoption surveys, and regulatory advocacy for the credit union industry. Credit Union National Awareness data and annual surveys are primary sources for CU technology trends. Member CUs receive research as part of dues. NOTE: Trade association and research organization, not a technology vendor.

Annual bank technology, AI adoption, and economic outlook surveys covering banks of all sizes. ABA Banking Journal and ABA Endorsed Solutions program shape community bank technology decisions. Research is member-free or low-cost. NOTE: Trade association and research organization, not a technology vendor.

Industry association formed from the 2024 merger of BAI and RMA; provides financial services leaders with benchmarking research, compliance training, enterprise risk and compliance software, and peer community resources; solution areas include Membership, Peer Sharing & Events, Compliance & Training, and Decision Support Solutions (Policy Manager, Enterprise Risk Manager, Board Manager); serves banks and credit unions of all sizes with focus on compliance officers, risk managers, and executives.

Demo-format fintech conferences (FinovateFall, FinovateSpring, FinovateEurope) where AI and fintech vendors pitch to FI buyers and analysts. Best of Show awards are a recognized signal of community FI market interest. Research content and conference coverage supplement vendor discovery. NOTE: Conference/research organization, not a technology vendor.

Community bank-focused research, regulatory advocacy, and fintech vetting through the ThinkTECH Accelerator. ThinkTECH cohort selections are a trusted signal for community bank technology buyers evaluating AI and fintech vendors. Annual community banking survey tracks technology investment and AI adoption. NOTE: Trade association and research organization, not a technology vendor.

Subscription-based FI technology research covering AI, payments, core banking, digital transformation, and risk. Community banks and larger regional FIs subscribe for analyst access, vendor evaluations, and benchmark studies. Reports are widely referenced in RFP processes and board presentations. NOTE: Research/advisory organization, not a technology vendor.

Subscription research and advisory covering banking technology, fraud, payments, lending, and insurance. Successor to Aite-Novarica; retains deep bench of former banker analysts. Community and regional banks subscribe for AI and technology benchmark studies. NOTE: Research/advisory organization, not a technology vendor.

Strategy research, technology selection advisory, and performance benchmarking for community banks and credit unions. "What's Going On in Banking" annual report is a primary source for technology adoption trends at FIs under $10B. Consulting engagements cover core conversions, digital banking strategy, and AI readiness. NOTE: Research/advisory organization, not a technology vendor.

Primary research, innovation labs, and incubation programs focused exclusively on credit unions. Research covers AI adoption, financial inclusion, product innovation, and member experience. i3 program embeds researchers at CUs for applied research. NOTE: Research/advisory organization, not a technology vendor. Credit union focus only.
Data and analytics platform covering US banks and credit unions; maps deployed technologies, financial performance, and offering benchmarks for vendor prospecting and competitive gap analysis.

AI-powered market intelligence platform covering startup funding, M&A, technology trends, and competitive landscape. Banking innovation teams use CB Insights to track AI vendor landscapes, monitor competitor moves, and identify emerging fintech categories. Fintech 250 and Banking AI reports are frequently referenced. NOTE: Market intelligence platform, not an FI technology vendor.

Competitive intelligence service that tracks direct mail and digital marketing campaigns across banking, credit cards, and financial services; provides marketers visibility into competitor offers and messaging.

categories.name: Payments & Digital Assets
categories.description: Vendors providing modern payment rails, faster payment access, stablecoin infrastructure, and digital asset services to community banks, credit unions, and fintechs.

subcategories.name: Payment Rails & Faster Payments Infrastructure
subcategories.description: Payment rails and faster payments infrastructure vendors provide API-first access to ACH origination, Real-Time Payments (RTP), FedNow, push-to-card (Visa Direct, Mastercard Send), and multi-rail payment orchestration for community financial institutions and fintechs building payment products. Products range from direct rail access APIs to full-service payment hubs with multi-rail routing intelligence. Payments strategy leaders, CTOs, and Treasury product managers are the buyers.
subcategories.ai_opportunities: AI-powered payment routing intelligence that dynamically selects the optimal rail (RTP, FedNow, ACH same-day, push-to-card) based on cost, speed, recipient capability, and fraud risk in real time is enabling community institutions to offer modern payment experiences without building custom multi-rail integration logic from scratch.

products.description: Real-time P2P and small business payment network embedded in FI mobile banking apps. Community banks and credit unions integrate Zelle through white-label digital banking providers (Q2, Alkami, etc.) or directly via API. 2,200+ participating FIs.

Real-time payments network enabling 24/7/365 instant credit transfers up to $10M. Community banks access RTP directly or through a correspondent bank or technology intermediary. Competing network to FedNow. Over 400 financial institutions connected as of 2024.
Payments hub aggregating ACH origination, FedNow, RTP (The Clearing House), wire, and Zelle for community FIs on Jack Henry cores. Includes iPay Technologies bill pay integration.

Payrailz is Jack Henry's digital payments platform offering bill pay, person-to-person payments, and account-to-account transfers for bank and credit union customers. It uses AI to surface payment insights and help financial institutions deliver a smarter, more personalized payments experience.

Consumer and commercial check deposit via smartphone imaging and desktop scanners; integrates with Jack Henry cores and digital banking platforms. Includes both consumer and business-tier RDC variants.

Real-time payments platform providing multi-network connectivity (FedNow, RTP, and stablecoin) for financial institutions; helps FIs deliver instant payment services to customers, grow fee income through premium treasury services, and streamline back-office operations.
On-us ATM posting to KeyStone core eliminating EFT network fees, with direct core integration for full account information and transactions at ATMs.
Payments hub for community FIs covering FedNow, RTP, ACH, and wire orchestration. Enables community banks to access faster payment rails without building direct integrations.
In-person and self-service instant debit/credit card issuance solution for FIs, including personalization hardware and software.
Real-time money movement service democratizing instant payments for community FIs, with RTP and FedNow connectivity.
SMS-based loan payment and account funding platform offering two-tap mobile payments, web pay, and phone pay for credit union members.

Cloud-based omnichannel bill payment platform enabling financial institutions to offer customers a unified bill payment experience across web, mobile, IVR, and agent channels; supports multiple payment methods with real-time posting and integrates with FI digital banking environments.
Remote deposit capture and payment orchestration platform for FIs covering mobile/desktop RDC, fraud risk management, instant payments via FedNow, ACH, and wire transfer.
Payment orchestration and gateway connectivity module of the Mambu platform; connects core banking to payment rails and processors; API-first; separate Payments Gateway API (MPG) available.

Multi-rail payment API platform covering ACH, RTP, FedNow, card issuing, and push-to-debit. Community banks use Moov to offer embedded payment services to business clients or to power fintech programs. No proprietary ML — infrastructure layer.

API-first ACH and pay-by-bank platform. Platforms embed Dwolla to move money via ACH, same-day ACH, and RTP. Community banks can serve as the partner bank in Dwolla's white-label model, processing ACH volume for fintech clients.
Payments orchestration platform covering ACH, wire, SWIFT messaging, and faster payments (FedNow/RTP) for community and regional banks. Consolidates payment rails into a single hub.
Cloud-based unified payments platform supporting FedNow, ACH, and wire transfers for financial institutions; among first FedNow-certified processors.

Elan Financial Services provides agent credit card program management for banks and credit unions; FIs outsource their credit card portfolio to Elan, which handles card issuing, credit underwriting, servicing, rewards, and fraud management under the FI's brand.
Open API cloud platform enabling FIs to control and extend digital money movement capabilities across any application or device.
Full-service payment processing including card, ACH, IVR, text pay, EBPP, and white-labeled member-facing payment portals for credit unions.

Independent EFT network and payments CUSO serving community banks and credit unions; provides debit card processing, ATM network access, fraud monitoring, instant payments, and payment processing services as a member-owned cooperative alternative to the major networks.
Full-service debit, credit, prepaid, ATM and mobile card solutions; nation-only Visa Debit Processing Service aggregator for credit unions.
Next-generation deposit service suite and digital payment solutions for credit unions, including check capture and faster payments channels.
International FX and domestic wire platform providing foreign currency exchange, international wires, and foreign check clearing for 4000+ FIs.
Card-on-file automation platform that places FI debit and credit cards as the default payment method at 142+ online merchants, driving top-of-wallet status and interchange revenue.

Multi-rail payment orchestration with real-time ledgering, automated reconciliation, and approval workflows. Connects to ACH, wire, RTP, and FedNow via bank partners. FIs use it to run Treasury operations and to offer embedded payment infrastructure to corporate clients.

Real-time B2B payment network using private blockchain for intraday and 24/7 settlement. Banks deploy TassatPay to offer corporate clients instant treasury settlement without relying on Fed windows. Also has a tokenized deposit angle for intrabank settlement.

Money API for fintech companies building payment applications on bank and credit union infrastructure. Supports ACH debit/credit, instant ACH, digital wallets, and bank-to-bank transfers. KYC/AML and digital wallet capabilities included.
Broad debit/credit processing, integrated payments, and B2B payment solutions processing 40B+ transactions annually across 146 countries.
Industry-leading instant card issuance solution with integrated hardware, software, hosting, and services; invented instant issuance 20+ years ago.
Checks, payment solutions, and business technology for financial institutions including fraud management and marketing services.
Payment solution enabling banks to remotely print and process official checks with full SilverLake System integration via jXchange for automated check workflow.
Card-on-file switching API enabling bank customers to update their payment card at merchant subscription and billing sites directly from within their digital banking app.
Real-time payment solution embedded in digital banking enabling instant account-to-account transfers and disbursements for community financial institutions.

Global B2B payments platform enabling businesses to send and receive payments internationally via local bank transfers, ACH, wire, and digital wallets; helps financial institutions offer business customers a modern multi-rail payments experience for domestic and cross-border transactions.
Instant payment infrastructure solution enabling community FIs to access RTP and FedNow payment rails through a single integration within digital banking.

Analytics platform for card issuers and acquirers to track Visa/Mastercard network fees, interchange qualification, and compliance; identifies potential network fee savings and cost reduction opportunities.
Enterprise content management with workflow automation, eForms, and record retention for credit unions integrated with Corelation KeyStone.
BaaS sponsor bank providing turnkey prepaid card, payments, and specialty lending programs via its regulatory infrastructure; formerly MetaBank, rebranded 2022.
Customer communications management and engagement platform for credit unions with personalized statements, cross-sell, and digital delivery.
Payroll connectivity API enabling frictionless direct deposit switching with 100% U.S. payroll coverage, including credential-less PreMatch technology for income verification.

Sidecar banking OS with open APIs for payments (FedNow, RTP, ACH, SWIFT, wires), accounts, FX, and general ledger; sits alongside legacy cores to modernize payment infrastructure; direct integrations with FIS and Fiserv; enables FIs to plug fintech AI tools into payment and account workflows

Core banking platform (formerly T24). Real-time, cloud-native, API-first core banking system. US community bank clients include TS Banking Group ($1.5B, Iowa — selected 2022), AimBank, Clare Bank, Farmers & Merchants Bank, National Bank of Andrews, State Bank of Spencer. Large US bank clients include Commerce Bank ($36.7B, core + loan origination + FedNow live 2022-2024) and Regions Bank ($155B, selected 2023 for deposit system). Delivered via Temenos Banking Cloud (SaaS) or on-premise.
Digital commercial escrow and subaccounting platform (ZEscrow) enabling FIs to offer IOLTA, real estate, and business escrow management within digital banking.

Coinbax Programmable Payments is a digital payment infrastructure platform that enables financial institutions and fintechs to build and deploy automated payment workflows. It supports programmable money movement logic for use cases such as escrow, disbursements, and conditional transfers.

subcategories.name: Stablecoins & Tokenized Deposits
subcategories.description: Stablecoins and tokenized deposit platforms include stablecoin issuers, tokenized deposit infrastructure providers, and digital asset custody solutions that have documented relationships with community banks, credit unions, or their sponsor bank partners. Products address institutional use cases including cross-border payments, treasury settlement, and digital dollar programs. CEOs, Chief Strategy Officers, and Treasury leaders at forward-leaning community institutions are the buyers.
subcategories.ai_opportunities: AI-assisted compliance monitoring and transaction surveillance specifically designed for blockchain-based payment flows is emerging as a necessary capability for community financial institutions that custody digital assets or process stablecoin transactions, where traditional BSA/AML rules-based systems are poorly calibrated for on-chain activity patterns.

products.description: White-label Bitcoin platform embedded in community bank and credit union mobile apps. Customers buy, hold, and sell Bitcoin through the FI's existing app; NYDIG handles custody, compliance, and trade execution. 300+ documented FI partners including community banks and credit unions.

Circle Mint enables regulated financial institutions to issue and redeem USDC directly, with 1:1 USD reserves held at regulated custodians. Community banks use Circle's API to integrate USDC settlement and stablecoin-based payments into treasury and cross-border products. Directly relevant under the GENIUS Act framework for bank-issued stablecoins.

Institutional-grade digital asset custody, transfer, and settlement platform using MPC (multi-party computation) key management. Banks deploy Fireblocks to custody crypto assets for clients, manage stablecoin reserves, and run tokenized deposit pilots. Integrates with Circle, Paxos, and major blockchain networks.

Institutional stablecoin issuance and settlement infrastructure. FIs use Paxos to issue white-label stablecoins, settle securities transactions on blockchain (Paxos Settlement Service), and access regulated custody. PYUSD (PayPal USD) runs on Paxos rails. Relevant for community banks exploring tokenized deposits under GENIUS Act.

Uses Provenance Blockchain to originate, fund, and securitize HELOCs and mortgages on-chain. Community banks partner with Figure to access a blockchain-native secondary market for loans. Also enables tokenized deposit experiments via Provenance. Bridges traditional lending and tokenization.

Federally chartered digital asset bank offering institutional custody, staking, financing, and on-chain governance participation. Community banks evaluating crypto custody can reference Anchorage as the OCC-approved model. Direct community bank adoption limited; primarily a benchmark and potential custody partner.

Coinbax Programmable Payments is a digital payment infrastructure platform that enables financial institutions and fintechs to build and deploy automated payment workflows. It supports programmable money movement logic for use cases such as escrow, disbursements, and conditional transfers.

Digital asset core enabling banks and credit unions to offer stablecoins, tokenized deposits, and digital asset products within their existing core ecosystems; integrates with SilverLake via jXchange and Symitar via SymXchange.

Real-time B2B payment network using private blockchain for intraday and 24/7 settlement. Banks deploy TassatPay to offer corporate clients instant treasury settlement without relying on Fed windows. Also has a tokenized deposit angle for intrabank settlement.

Banking-as-a-Service platform enabling community banks to run compliant fintech sponsor bank programs. Provides ledger, KYC/AML, card issuance, money movement, compliance monitoring, fraud controls, and dispute management via API. Centralizes risk management for sponsor banks through Synctera Console. Notable sponsor bank clients: Coastal Community Bank (27 fintech partnerships), Regent Bank.

Stablecoin-powered international payment and FX platform for credit unions; uses a stablecoin sandwich architecture (local currency in, USDC intermediary, USD out via domestic wire) achieving sub-10-minute settlement at 4-10% lower cost than SWIFT; targeted at CUs serving international students and immigrant communities; backed by a16z; partnered with MSU Federal Credit Union and Lake Michigan Credit Union; $2M processed in first month of operation

Multi-tenant stablecoin treasury and digital asset infrastructure platform for financial institutions; configurable fee tiers, mass disbursement, invoice generation, and white-label deployment; enables FIs to issue, manage, and settle stablecoins without building proprietary infrastructure; positions against $46T in global stablecoin volume (3x Visa) and monthly volume now exceeding ACH in the US

Conditional payment infrastructure using stablecoins to solve counterparty trust problems in peer-to-peer and commercial transactions; funds are released only when defined conditions are met; designed for agentic commerce scenarios where programmable payment logic replaces manual escrow or trust arrangements

categories.name: Enterprise & Horizontal Technology
categories.description: Horizontal enterprise technology — CRM, productivity, and AI copilot tools — that financial institution staff use across departments regardless of banking function.

subcategories.name: CRM & Sales Enablement
subcategories.description: CRM and sales enablement tools provide financial institution relationship managers, branch staff, and business banking teams with pipeline management, contact tracking, opportunity management, and sales activity reporting to grow loans, deposits, and non-interest income. Products range from banking-specific CRM platforms to configured versions of general-purpose CRMs. Retail banking heads, commercial banking leaders, and COOs are the buyers.
subcategories.ai_opportunities: AI-powered opportunity scoring within CRM platforms — which surfaces which customers or prospects are most likely to need a specific product based on life event signals and transaction behavior — is significantly improving sales productivity for community institution relationship managers who manage large books without dedicated analyst support.

products.description: CRM and customer engagement platform built on Microsoft Dynamics 365 for retail, SME, and corporate banks; covers onboarding, sales, contact center, complaints, and loyalty management.

CRM and analytics platform for financial institutions with AI-powered churn prediction and member retention capabilities; Einstein Prediction Builder enables FIs to build custom attrition propensity models on customer relationship data; Einstein Next Best Action delivers AI-driven retention intervention recommendations to relationship managers; integrated with marketing automation for proactive retention outreach; banking-specific data models for households, financial goals, and product relationships; used by community banks and credit unions for relationship banking, member engagement, and retention program management
Banking CRM platform covering retail and commercial digital journeys, loan origination, KYC, and next-best-action recommendation.
No-code CRM and workflow automation platform with banking use cases for front and middle office; covers customer onboarding, lending, deposit accounts, contact center, and compliance workflows.

Cloud-based CRM platform tracking leads, contacts, and deals through a unified pipeline; deployed by community banks and credit unions for business development activities, marketing automation, and prospect outreach.

Unified, straight-through digital experience platform for banks covering retail, commercial, and wealth management from client onboarding through operations; integrates with SilverLake, Yellow Hammer, and Synergy via jXchange.
Appointment scheduling and virtual engagement platform for financial institutions enabling customers to book branch appointments, video calls, and digital consultations from digital banking.

subcategories.name: Productivity & Collaboration
subcategories.description: Productivity and collaboration platforms provide office productivity suites, unified communications, videoconferencing, document management, and collaboration tools used by financial institution staff across all departments. Products in this category are horizontal technology purchases driven by IT and operations, though increasingly with security and compliance overlays required for banking environments. CTOs, COOs, and IT managers are the buyers.
subcategories.ai_opportunities: Microsoft 365 Copilot and similar AI productivity tools embedded in workplace applications are creating significant efficiency opportunities for community bank and credit union staff across compliance documentation, meeting summarization, and internal reporting workflows — with governance frameworks now emerging to guide secure FI deployment.

products.description: Strategic IT consulting, assessments, strategic roadmaps, and managed services for credit unions seeking technology competitive advantage.
IT CUSO providing KeyStone development, consulting, disaster recovery, infrastructure hosting, and managed services via Community Cloud.
KeyStone service bureau combining core hosting with digital banking, disaster recovery, and custom KeyStone programming for 30+ CUs.
IT solutions and managed services for credit unions including system integration, process automation, and infrastructure transformation.
Multi-line insurance agency/brokerage providing insurance products to credit unions across the country for over a decade.

RingCentral MVP (Message, Video, Phone) is a cloud-based unified communications platform used by financial institutions to consolidate business messaging, video conferencing, and phone systems. It integrates with existing workflows and enables staff to communicate securely across desktop and mobile devices.
Disaster recovery, private cloud backup, hosting, and data center colocation services exclusively for credit unions since 1998.
SaaS data protection platform for Salesforce providing backup, recovery, archiving, and sandbox seeding; protects nCino/Salesforce banking data for FIs.

Integrated contact center platform offering omni-channel communication (voice, digital), IVR, AI-powered automation, agent and supervisor management tools, and cloud hosting. Pre-built integrations with banking core systems. Quantified efficiency gains: 40 seconds saved per call via pre-authentication and 60 seconds per call with ProSpeak.
Learning experience platform for financial institutions; delivers employee onboarding, compliance training, product knowledge, and customer digital education.

AI-powered knowledge management platform with certified search for regulated industries; automatically detects and resolves conflicting, duplicative, or outdated policies in a knowledge base; prevents authoritative-sounding but incorrect AI outputs that result from organizational knowledge disorder; designed for compliance training verification and policy management at FIs

subcategories.name: Productivity Tools
subcategories.description: AI productivity tools for financial institutions include enterprise AI assistants, banking-specific copilot applications, and generative AI platforms deployed across FI staff for tasks such as drafting communications, summarizing documents, generating compliance reports, analyzing contracts, and supporting internal knowledge management. Products range from general-purpose LLM deployments to purpose-built banking AI assistants. CEOs, CTOs, and department heads across the institution are buyers.
subcategories.ai_opportunities: Purpose-built banking AI assistants that are pre-trained on regulatory guidance, core system documentation, and banking workflows are delivering measurable productivity gains for community bank staff — particularly in compliance, credit analysis, and customer-facing roles — while maintaining the data security posture required in a regulated environment.

products.description: Multi-channel LLM-based agentic AI system for credit unions providing intelligent self-service and internal automation across 500+ FI brands.

AI assistant integrated across Microsoft 365 applications (Word, Excel, Outlook, Teams, PowerPoint) that helps bank and credit union staff draft documents, summarize meetings, analyze data, and automate repetitive tasks; deployed by FIs for operational productivity under enterprise security and compliance controls.

ChatGPT Enterprise is OpenAI's commercial offering of ChatGPT with enterprise-grade security, privacy, and administrative controls. Financial institutions deploy it to give employees access to a capable generative AI assistant while keeping data isolated from model training.

AI workflow automation agents for banks and credit unions. Automates lending operations spanning consumer, mortgage, and commercial, quality control, and back-office tasks end-to-end from inbox to core to compliance. Includes human supervision checkpoints and full audit trails for exam readiness.

Hapax is an AI platform purpose-built for financial institutions, offering tools for document processing, policy Q&A, and workflow automation trained on banking-specific content. It enables banks and credit unions to deploy AI assistants for staff and members without exposing sensitive data to public models.

Private AI governance platform for financial institutions providing 100+ pre-built AI agents with built-in GRC and cybersecurity controls, complete audit trails, and full data sovereignty. Positioned for banks, credit unions, and wealth managers seeking to deploy AI without sharing data with third-party model providers.

categories.name: Core Banking & Financial Infrastructure
categories.description: The foundational core banking systems and modern cloud-native alternatives that serve as the central ledger and system of record for community and regional financial institutions.

subcategories.name: Traditional Core Banking Systems
subcategories.description: Traditional core banking systems are the established, proven platforms that serve as the central ledger and system of record for the majority of community and regional banks and credit unions in the United States, including FIS IBS/Horizon, Fiserv DNA/Precision/Premier, Jack Henry SilverLake/Banno, and CSI NuPoint. These systems manage accounts, transactions, general ledger, and regulatory reporting and have decades of accumulated FI-specific functionality. CEOs, COOs, and IT leaders making long-term technology commitments are the primary decision-makers.
subcategories.ai_opportunities: Core banking vendors are increasingly embedding AI capabilities — AI-powered fraud alerts, intelligent teller workflows, and predictive analytics — directly into their platforms through native development and fintech partnerships, making the core system itself an AI delivery channel for community institutions that have limited appetite for point solution proliferation.

products.description: Fiserv flagship core banking platform, the most widely deployed community bank core in the US. SQL-based open architecture. Serves community banks from ~$200M to several billion in assets.
Fiserv core banking platform for smaller community banks. Simplified feature set relative to Premier, designed for institutions under $500M assets.
Fiserv core banking platform targeting larger community banks and smaller regional banks, typically $1B+ in assets. More complex feature set than Premier, with stronger trust and wealth capabilities.
FIS flagship core banking platform for mid-size community banks. Full-service core ledger with integrated teller, platform, and back-office capabilities. Competes directly with Fiserv Premier.

FIS core banking platform for community and mid-tier banks; provides real-time deposit and loan account processing, integrated teller and platform operations, and back-office capabilities with a modern service-oriented architecture.
Jack Henry flagship core banking platform for community banks with assets generally above $500M. SQL-based open architecture. Dominant in the mid-to-larger community bank tier.
Jack Henry core banking platform targeting smaller community banks (typically under $500M assets). Simpler feature set than SilverLake, lower implementation cost.

CIF 2020 is Jack Henry's customer information file solution that provides community banks with a centralized repository of customer and account data. It serves as a foundational data layer for relationship management and cross-sell initiatives across the institution.

Core Director is Jack Henry's core banking platform designed for community banks, providing deposit and loan account processing, teller operations, and back-office banking functions. It is one of the primary core platforms in Jack Henry's portfolio for smaller community institutions.

Comprehensive community bank technology platform covering core banking (NuPoint), digital banking, payments processing, regulatory compliance, fraud prevention, cybersecurity managed services, and open banking APIs; AI-powered fraud and AML compliance tools; one of the largest fintech and regtech providers serving the community bank segment in the U.S.; single-vendor suite enabling community banks to consolidate technology relationships
Partner integration marketplace for CSI core banking clients; 67 vetted fintech solutions across lending, payments, digital banking, compliance, and card services.

Automated lineage mapping and modernization platform for COBOL, RPG, and PL/1 banking cores; produces a continuously updated dependency map of legacy systems to support safe AI deployment, regulatory data lineage requirements, and core migration planning; won Best in Show at FinovateSpring 2026

Automated architecture discovery and dependency mapping platform for financial institution IT environments; produces a continuously updated map of all existing systems, integrations, and dependencies that has never previously existed; designed for FIs planning to deploy AI agents, modernize legacy cores, or respond to regulatory data lineage requirements; solves the blast radius problem where nobody knows what depends on a 30-year-old system before changing it

subcategories.name: Cloud-Native & Modern Core Banking
subcategories.description: Cloud-native and modern core banking platforms are next-generation systems built on cloud-native architectures with API-first designs, real-time processing, and composable product configurations — including Mambu, Thought Machine Vault, Temenos Transact, and Finxact. These platforms target financial institutions seeking to modernize away from legacy mainframe-era cores or launch new digital bank entities. CEOs, boards, and technology transformation leaders at institutions willing to undertake significant migration projects are the buyers.
subcategories.ai_opportunities: Cloud-native cores with real-time data architectures and open APIs are dramatically more compatible with AI integration than legacy batch-processing cores, enabling institutions that migrate to modern platforms to deploy AI use cases in weeks rather than the months-long integration projects required against traditional core systems.

products.description: Cloud-native, API-first core banking SaaS platform; enables financial institutions to configure banking products, integrate ecosystem partners, and evolve continuously on a composable architecture without legacy technology constraints; supports consumer, SME, and embedded finance use cases.

Cloud-native modern core banking platform acquired by Fiserv in 2021. Built on AWS with a real-time ledger and open API layer. Positioned as Fiserv next-generation core for forward-looking community FIs.
Cloud-native core banking platform built on Microsoft Azure. FIS next-generation architecture for community FIs seeking to modernize. Real-time processing with open API design.

Core banking platform (formerly T24). Real-time, cloud-native, API-first core banking system. US community bank clients include TS Banking Group ($1.5B, Iowa — selected 2022), AimBank, Clare Bank, Farmers & Merchants Bank, National Bank of Andrews, State Bank of Spencer. Large US bank clients include Commerce Bank ($36.7B, core + loan origination + FedNow live 2022-2024) and Regions Bank ($155B, selected 2023 for deposit system). Delivered via Temenos Banking Cloud (SaaS) or on-premise.

Cloud-native, API-first core banking platform on Google Cloud; modular architecture covering deposit management, ACH, card, wires, and general ledger. FIs adopt components incrementally alongside existing Jack Henry cores.

Cloud-native core banking system purpose-built for community banks. Consolidates core processing, CRM, compliance, and analytics into one integrated platform positioned as an alternative to multi-vendor stacks from large core providers. API-first architecture built for modern integration patterns.

Cloud-native, API-first alternative core banking platform for community banks and credit unions. Enables full core conversion or co-existence alongside legacy cores. Supports digital brand studio (Nymbus Labs) for launching niche banking brands and BaaS programs via CUSO model. Notable 2025 milestone: PeoplesBank conversion (largest modern core conversion by a US community bank). Also supports MSU FCU, Chrome FCU, Vantage West CU.

AI-native banking platform with embedded ML via the CLARA AI engine for credit modeling, personalization, and fraud and AML detection. Includes a no-code policy engine for building and testing credit models without data scientists.

subcategories.name: Credit Union Core Systems
subcategories.description: Credit union core systems are purpose-built core banking platforms designed specifically for the cooperative structure, share account model, and member service culture of credit unions — including Symitar (Jack Henry), Corelation Keystone, CU*Answers CU*BASE, Sharetec, and others. These platforms handle shares, loans, membership, ACH, and regulatory reporting within the NCUA regulatory framework. CEOs, COOs, and technology leadership at credit unions are the decision-makers.
subcategories.ai_opportunities: AI capabilities being built into credit union core platforms — including member behavior analytics, automated loan decisioning, and conversational AI integration — are enabling CUs to deliver the personalized, high-touch member experience that defines their competitive differentiation, now at digital scale and without proportional staff increases.

products.description: Jack Henry core banking platform purpose-built for credit unions. The dominant credit union core in the US credit union market, serving institutions of all sizes.

Open-architecture credit union core banking platform. Built by ex-Symitar engineers; launched 2009. Features real-time processing, open database access, and a configurable data model attractive to mid-size CUs seeking flexibility over packaged systems.

Core banking platform (formerly T24); real-time, cloud-native, API-first core banking system; delivered via Temenos Banking Cloud (SaaS) or on-premise; deployed at community banks and regional banks in the US and internationally.
Loan origination, collections & recovery, and account origination platform for credit unions and community banks; formerly Akcelerant (acquired by Temenos Feb 2014); cloud-native SaaS delivery.

categories.name: Digital Banking Platforms
categories.description: Consumer and business-facing online and mobile banking platforms, plus digital account opening solutions, that form the primary digital touchpoints for FI customers and members.

subcategories.name: Retail Digital Banking Platforms
subcategories.description: Retail digital banking platforms provide the consumer-facing online and mobile banking experiences through which retail customers and members manage accounts, make payments, apply for products, and interact with their financial institution — including Q2, Alkami, Narmi, Candescent/NCR, and Apiture. These platforms are the primary digital touchpoint for the institution and integrate with core systems, fintech partners, and payment networks. CEOs, Digital Banking VPs, and COOs are the buyers.
subcategories.ai_opportunities: AI-powered personalization and embedded financial wellness features within retail digital banking platforms — such as proactive spending insights, savings round-up prompts, and AI chat support — are becoming key competitive differentiators for community institutions seeking to match the experience quality of neobanks and large bank apps.

products.description: Intelligent Banking Platform (formerly NCR Voyix Digital Banking) for banks and credit unions; unifies data and insight to deliver smarter, more personalized digital experiences across retail and business banking channels with 60+ marketplace partner integrations.

Jack Henry integrated digital banking platform covering mobile, online banking, and banker tools. Used by both community banks (on SilverLake/CruiseNet) and credit unions (on Symitar). Tight core integration is key differentiator.

Open services digital banking platform from Fiserv with a modular framework and configurable SDK, enabling banks and credit unions to build distinct retail and small business digital experiences across online, mobile, and tablet channels. Features 500+ pre-packaged modules, an AppMarket of Fiserv and third-party integrations, and a collaborative developer community for institutional code-sharing; core-neutral architecture supports both Fiserv and non-Fiserv cores. Institutions choose hosted or on-premise deployment and can extend the platform via the optional Architect SDK. Serves hundreds of community banks and credit unions; Fiserv is evolving the platform under the Create Digital branding while maintaining continuity for existing Architect clients.
Cloud-based digital banking platform with embedded AI-driven analytics, predictive product recommendations, and personalization for community and mid-tier FIs

Integrated digital banking platform from FIS serving regional and community banks with consistent omnichannel experiences across retail, business, and branch channels. Modular ecosystem covers online banking, mobile banking, online account opening, business banking, teller processing, and Financial Wellness Suite tools including AI-powered spending insights and personalization; pre-integrated with FIS core banking solutions and open to third-party APIs via Digital One APIs. Designed for rapid out-of-the-box deployment with minimal configuration; modules can be adopted incrementally or as a full deployment. Single-vendor alternative to piecemeal digital banking solutions, with managed security, risk, and compliance built in.
Enterprise digital experience and content management platform used by large banks to deliver personalized web and mobile banking portals, content optimization, and omnichannel customer journeys.

Unified digital banking and account opening platform for community banks and credit unions. Suite includes Narmi Banking (consumer/business digital banking), Narmi Grow (account opening), Narmi Now (FedNow real-time payments), Narmi Guard (fraud prevention), Narmi Command (digital back-office), Narmi Open (API-driven open banking), Narmi Lend (digital lending), and Narmi AI. Cloud-native, API-first architecture.
End-to-end digital banking suite including mobile, online, account opening, loan origination, and AI voice banking integrated with KeyStone.

All-in-one digital presence platform for community banks and credit unions; provides ADA-compliant branded websites, live chat, digital marketing (SEO/SEM/social), and FFIEC-aligned cybersecurity risk assessments.

Cloud-native commercial banking platform for digital transformation of treasury management. Automates account opening, treasury onboarding, data exchange, and compliance with a developer-first API portal for community bank integration teams.

White-label teen and youth banking platform for financial institutions; distributed through a network of 2,200 schools; 300,000 active accounts; guaranteed volume model for FI partners; generates 37 transactions per month per account; captures the next generation of primary banking relationships before competitors

subcategories.name: Business & Commercial Digital Banking
subcategories.description: Business and commercial digital banking platforms provide small business and commercial banking clients with online and mobile access to treasury services, cash management, ACH origination, wire transfers, business account management, and commercial lending portals. Products range from business modules within retail digital banking platforms to purpose-built commercial banking portals. Commercial banking leaders, Treasury product managers, and COOs are the buyers.
subcategories.ai_opportunities: AI-powered cash flow forecasting, anomaly alerts, and intelligent payment recommendations delivered within the business digital banking experience are significant opportunities for community institutions to deepen commercial relationships by providing treasury intelligence capabilities previously available only through expensive treasury management consultants.

products.description: Commercial cash management platform for mid-to-large business clients; covers ACH origination, domestic and international wires, bulk payment uploads, multi-user approval controls, and account reporting.

Fully white-labeled treasury management and business digital banking platform for credit unions and community banks. Includes ACH, wire, payroll, cash management, and role-based entitlements. Pre-integrated with most major core systems and designed for FIs that want to compete with regional bank commercial offerings.

Cloud-native commercial banking platform for digital transformation of treasury management. Automates account opening, treasury onboarding, data exchange, and compliance with a developer-first API portal for community bank integration teams.

API-first digital banking and open banking integration platform for community banks and credit unions; enables connectivity between fintech vendors and core banking systems (FIS, Fiserv, Jack Henry, CSI); 40+ core integrations; Fintech Connector module for B2B FI-to-vendor API management
End-to-end digital banking suite including mobile, online, account opening, loan origination, and AI voice banking integrated with KeyStone.

subcategories.name: Digital Account Opening
subcategories.description: Digital account opening platforms provide standalone or integrated solutions for opening consumer checking, savings, CD, and money market accounts — and in some cases business accounts — entirely through digital channels, handling identity verification, KYC, document capture, funding, and core system booking. Products address the critical onboarding experience for both new and existing customers. Digital banking leaders, retail banking heads, and COOs are the buyers.
subcategories.ai_opportunities: AI-powered identity verification and fraud detection embedded directly in the account opening flow is enabling community financial institutions to approve legitimate applicants in under three minutes while maintaining CIP compliance and fraud loss rates competitive with large bank digital channels.

products.description: Self-service digital account opening for consumer and business deposits; biometric ID verification, document OCR, and under-5-minute application flow. Integrates with all four Jack Henry cores.

Credit union-focused digital account opening platform integrated with loan origination via arc OS and arc DX. Streamlines membership account opening in under six minutes with fraud detection, application pre-fill, and a unified origination experience across loans and deposits. Rebranded from CU Direct.

Teller transaction processing, branch deposit operations, and omnichannel account opening software for banks and credit unions; covers real-time fraud detection at the teller line and BSA/AML screening.
Pre-sales digital engagement platform for banks and credit unions; delivers product selection tools, financial calculators, and account opening journeys for website visitors.

AI-native digital account opening platform purpose-built for credit unions and community banks. Real-time core integrations with Symitar, Corelation, Fiserv DNA, and Portico. AI-guided conversion flows and live in under four weeks at pricing designed for community FI budgets.
Banking CRM platform covering retail and commercial digital journeys, loan origination, KYC, and next-best-action recommendation.
Customer communications management and engagement platform for credit unions with personalized statements, cross-sell, and digital delivery.

Digital acquisition platform combining paid media, UX optimization, and conversion analytics for community banks and credit unions; identified that 39% of potential loan applications are lost from broken mobile application flows; targets measurable improvement in digital channel conversion rates

Business banking account activation infrastructure; automates the migration of payroll direct deposits, vendor payments, income sources, and recurring expenses from a prior bank to the new business account; converts an opened-but-unfunded account into a primary funded relationship; addresses the 60% of new business bank accounts that are opened and never funded, and the 5.4M businesses that attempt annual banking relationship switches with $72B in revenue at stake
